perturbation.original_text: 9.0 Each party hereto respectively appoints the below identified person as its Data Control Coordinator for the receipt and dispatch, on its behalf, of all PROPRIETARY information it discloses to the other party hereto pursuant to this Agreement. Each party reserves the right to change its Data Control Co-ordinator by giving the other party written notice thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.0 Each party hereto respectively appoints the below identified person as its Data Control Coordinator for the receipt and dispatch, on its behalf, of all PROPRIETARY information it discloses to the other party hereto pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence 'Each party reserves the right to change its Data Control Co-ordinator by giving the other party written notice thereof,' a condition about the Data Control Coordinator is omitted. If other sections refer to the process for changing the Data Control Coordinator, those references now lack context and are contradictory because the mechanism to change is deleted. This also creates a contradiction regarding the operational nature of the appointed 'Data Control Coordinator'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.0 Each party hereto respectively appoints the below identified person as its Data Control Coordinator for the receipt and dispatch, on its behalf, of all PROPRIETARY information it discloses to the other party hereto pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a mechanism to change the Data Control Coordinator with written notice. The changed text omits this mechanism. The contradicted text also appoints the Data Control Coordinator. Since the ability to change the coordinator is omitted, and the function of that coordinator is specified in both clauses, the changed clause introduces a contradiction because it becomes unclear how to change the Data Control Coordinator if needed, affecting the enforceability and operational consistency of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 10. In the event one party hereto orally discloses its PROPRIETARY information to the other party hereto, disclosing party agrees to promptly notify the Data Control Co-ordinator of the RECIPIENT of such oral disclosure, and reduce to writing each information within thirty (30) days of such oral disclosure and the names of the employees of RECIPIENT to whom such oral disclosure was made, including therein a brief description of the information disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. In the event one party hereto orally discloses information to the other party hereto, disclosing party agrees to promptly notify the Data Control Co-ordinator of the RECIPIENT of such oral disclosure, and reduce to writing each information within thirty (30) days of such oral disclosure and the names of the employees of RECIPIENT to whom such oral disclosure was made, including therein a brief description of the information disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word 'PROPRIETARY', the scope of oral disclosures that require notification is broadened to include all information, not just proprietary information. This creates a contradiction as the title of this legal document is a Non-Disclosure Agreement, the agreement is intended to limit disclosure of confidential information. The omission here creates confusion between the definition of PROPRIETARY and non PROPRIETARY. This might be considered a scope issue
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. In the event one party hereto orally discloses information to the other party hereto, disclosing party agrees to promptly notify the Data Control Co-ordinator of the RECIPIENT of such oral disclosure, and reduce to writing each information within thirty (30) days of such oral disclosure and the names of the employees of RECIPIENT to whom such oral disclosure was made, including therein a brief description of the information disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The original text limits the notification requirement to 'PROPRIETARY' information. The changed text removes 'PROPRIETARY', expanding the notification requirement to ALL information disclosed orally. This contradicts the fundamental nature of a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA), which is intended to protect only confidential/proprietary information, as indicated in the explanation provided. Since the NDA is no longer applicable to just proprietary information, it contradicts the nature of the contract itself. Therefore, the change introduces a clear and direct conflict, making it an in-text contradiction.
